core_mechanism: additive superposition of two independent perturbations onto a shared effective quantity, producing a many-to-one (degenerate, non-identifiable) map from distinct underlying causes to nearly identical observable output, with that degeneracy lifted only in the exponentially suppressed asymptotic (high-frequency) tail of the response spectrum
brief_reason: The abstract shows two distinct configurations exerting an 'additive enhancement' on the same observable (radiative efficiency) that makes them degenerate at low-to-intermediate frequencies, a degeneracy 'structurally broken only in the extreme Wien tail' of the spectrum.
